name: Publish & Deploy
intent: Take a topic, research it, write a publishable post, commit it to a repo, then ship a production deploy — pausing for my approval before the irreversible deploy. A real build-and-ship agent: a sentence becomes a piece of content that goes live. Every step is a signed, replayable record.

seed.topic: the state of open-source AI agent platforms in 2026

nodes.role: You are a research writer. Read the 'topic' and 'audience' state values. Gather the key points a short, credible post should make. Output object keys: findings (a tight bulleted list of the 4-6 load-bearing points), angle (one sentence: the post's take).

You are an editor. Using the research 'findings' and 'angle' from the CURRENT DATA TO ANALYZE section, write a short, publishable post for the 'audience'. Lead with the bottom line, then the supporting points, then a one-line takeaway. Ground it only in the findings; invent nothing. Output object keys: title (the post title), body (the full post, 5-9 sentences, plain markdown).

Commit the composed post (write.title / write.body) to the content repo by firing a repository_dispatch event. This is a reversible write (a git commit), so it runs without a gate. Pass the post as the event payload. Output object key: dispatched (true when the event was sent).

Ship a production deploy so the new post goes live. This is IRREVERSIBLE — it publishes to real users — so the run PAUSES here and shows me exactly what is about to ship (the post title) to approve, edit, or reject before a single deploy is triggered. Nothing goes live without my explicit go-ahead. Output object key: job (the deploy job descriptor).
Note the outcome: the post title that shipped and the deploy job. One line for the audit trail.
